channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This ecosystem allows developers to create more powerful and versatile AI solutions.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to engage attendees and highlight advancements in AI technology.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on learning about agentic AI, with specialized tracks on various tech topics. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour offers interactive sessions for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on experience. This initiative aims to provide practical knowledge on advanced topics like AI agents.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and custom commands. These enhancements aim to improve collaboration between developers and AI.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines, aim to reduce developer friction and improve code comprehension. These tools act as AI pair programmers to assist in coding tasks.

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman addressed an attack on his home and responded to a critical New Yorker article questioning his trustworthiness. His blog post aims to clarify his position amidst controversy.

item.title: How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk
item.description: Salesforce and ServiceNow are competing in the helpdesk market, with Salesforce focusing on user engagement and ServiceNow on AI governance. This rivalry highlights differing strategies in the tech industry.

item.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
item.description: The Ostation 2 Pro battery charger by Olight offers a solution for monitoring battery life, enhancing convenience for users of Olight flashlights. This product aims to simplify battery management.

item.title: Two different attackers poisoned popular open source tools - and showed us the future of supply chain compromise
item.description: Two supply chain attacks in March compromised popular open source tools, allowing attackers to steal sensitive information from numerous organizations. This incident highlights the growing threat of supply chain vulnerabilities in software development.

item.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
item.description: A controversial illustration of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman in The New Yorker has sparked discussions about the appropriateness of AI-generated art in journalism. The artwork features unsettling depictions of Altman, raising questions about representation.

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: Recent reports reveal that push notifications are not secure from FBI surveillance, raising concerns about privacy and data security. This comes amid other issues, such as an ongoing internet blackout in Iran and rising cryptocurrency scams.

item.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
item.description: A casual text about singer Mitski's father being a CIA operative sparked a humorous exploration of internet rumors. The article reflects on the absurdity of modern communication and the strange paths of information.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: The article discusses how the internet has made it increasingly difficult to discern truth from falsehood, particularly with the rise of AI-generated content. It emphasizes the challenges faced by verification systems.

item.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best augmented reality (AR) and mixed reality (MR) glasses available in 2026 highlights their potential for enhancing work and entertainment experiences. The article provides insights into user experiences and technology advancements.

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: The article compares the online strategies of the Iranian regime and the U.S. White House during a conflict, noting how Iran effectively used social media to convey its narrative. This highlights the power of information warfare.

item.title: Hungarian government creds left in the safe hands of 'FrankLampard'
item.description: Hungary's government faced a security breach when nearly 800 state login credentials were leaked, including sensitive defense accounts. This incident underscores the risks associated with poor password management.

item.title: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description: GitHub announced new limits and the retirement of a feature in Copilot Pro+ due to increased usage patterns. This change aims to manage high concurrency and improve overall service performance.

item.title: Snowflake manager explains the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI agent data access
item.description: A Snowflake manager discussed the importance of clean and accessible data for AI agents, suggesting that data quality is a key bottleneck in AI development. This perspective emphasizes the responsibility that comes with data access.

item.title: Here's how to watch the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned four astronauts after traveling farther from Earth than any humans before. This achievement marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description: GitHub has updated its metrics to aggregate active user counts for the newly renamed Copilot cloud agent, reflecting changes in its data schema.

item.title: Red Hat RHELocates its Chinese engineering team to India
item.description: Red Hat has relocated its Chinese engineering team to India, resulting in significant layoffs, reflecting a shift in geopolitical priorities rather than just downsizing.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: Anthropic has temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing Claude after a pricing change affected OpenClaw users.

item.title: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description: GitHub is pausing new trials for Copilot Pro due to increased abuse of its free trial system, aiming to protect legitimate users.

item.title: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description: GitHub has implemented a limit of 50 reruns for Actions workflows, introducing a failed check suite for attempts beyond this limit.

item.title: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description: Copilot CLI activity is now included in the overall usage metrics, enhancing the feature breakdowns for better insights.

item.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
item.description: A promotion offers a 50% discount on the Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver, ideal for home repairs and DIY projects.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their impact across various industries.

item.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
item.description: Home Depot is offering a Ryobi yard essentials bundle that includes a free power tool, promoting seasonal gardening and maintenance.

item.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
item.description: Jabra's Evolve3 75 headset is designed for business use, offering a boomless design that enhances professionalism in hybrid work settings. This innovation aims to improve audio quality while maintaining a sleek appearance.

item.title: I used this EcoFlow battery to run my 3,000-sq-ft home in a blackout - here's how it kept my AC on
item.description: The EcoFlow Delta Pro Ultra X battery can power a 3,000-sq-ft home during blackouts, maintaining essential functions like air conditioning. This device offers a reliable solution for homeowners seeking uninterrupted power.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, is raising concerns as it could be exploited by hackers, prompting a reevaluation of cybersecurity practices among developers. Experts warn that security must become a priority in AI development.

item.title: Microsoft's Windows Insider Program is no longer a confusing mess
item.description: Microsoft has revamped its Windows Insider Program, addressing previous confusion and introducing unexpected upgrades for Windows 11 users. This change aims to improve user experience and streamline feedback.

item.title: WATCH: Prices surged in March after oil shock set off by Iran war
item.description: Inflation surged in March due to an oil shock linked to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, aligning with economists' predictions. This economic shift highlights the ongoing impact of geopolitical events on markets.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.28.3.dev8
item.description: The ouroboros-ai 0.28.3.dev8 is a workflow engine designed for AI coding agents, compatible with Claude Code and Codex CLI. It emphasizes a specification-first approach to streamline AI development.

item.title: highfis 0.1.0
item.description: highFIS 0.1.0 is a new Python package for training high-dimensional TSK fuzzy systems, built on PyTorch and compatible with scikit-learn. This tool aims to enhance machine learning capabilities in fuzzy logic.

item.title: OpenAI says CEO Sam Altman's house was targeted with a Molotov cocktail
item.description: A suspect was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, raising security concerns for tech leaders. Fortunately, no injuries were reported in the incident.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.28.3.dev7
item.description: The ouroboros-ai 0.28.3.dev7 is another iteration of the workflow engine for AI coding agents, similar to its predecessor. It continues to support Claude Code and Codex CLI.

item.title: Sam Altman’s San Francisco mega-mansion hit with molotov cocktail
item.description: Reports confirm that a Molotov cocktail was thrown at the home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, leading to an arrest. This incident raises alarms about safety in the tech sector.

item.title: langchain-llama-server 0.1.2
item.description: LangChain has released version 0.1.2 of its llama-server, which enhances integrations for AI inference. This update is significant for developers using LangChain for AI applications.

item.title: Other Barks & Bites for Friday, April 10: DISH Wins Copyright Appeal Over Arabic TV Retransmissions; Fifth Circuit Awards Google Transfer on Mandamus; and Third Circuit Says Online Publication of Copyrighted Building Codes is Transformative
item.description: This week's legal updates include a copyright appeal victory for DISH and a significant AI cloud partnership extension between Meta and CoreWeave. These developments highlight ongoing legal and business dynamics in the tech industry.

item.title: Why more Mac attacks now rely on social engineering
item.description: Recent findings indicate a rise in social engineering attacks targeting Mac users, where attackers manipulate users into executing commands rather than exploiting software vulnerabilities. This shift underscores the need for heightened user awareness.

item.title: aichatclient 3.4.4
item.description: Version 3.4.4 of aichatclient has been released, providing a Python client for interacting with multiple AI models via OpenAI-compatible APIs. This tool simplifies access to various AI models for developers.

item.title: tensor-optix 1.1.0
item.description: The tensor-optix 1.1.0 update introduces an autonomous training loop for sequential learning models, featuring built-in algorithms for TensorFlow and PyTorch. This advancement streamlines the training process for machine learning practitioners.

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI Sues Colorado Over AI Law as Fight Over State Regulation Intensifies
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is suing Colorado over a new high-risk AI law, intensifying the debate over state regulation of AI technologies. This legal challenge reflects broader concerns about AI governance and accountability.

item.title: Talk Python to Me: #544: Wheel Next + Packaging PEPs
item.description: The Talk Python to Me podcast discusses issues with package installations that lack modern optimizations, highlighting challenges developers face with Python's packaging system. This conversation is crucial for improving software development practices.

item.title: Crypto Victims Relieved – Or Too Late? What Operation Atlantic Really Means
item.description: Operation Atlantic is a collaborative initiative aimed at combating crypto fraud and phishing attacks, showcasing a united front between crypto companies and authorities. This operation is a notable step in addressing rising cybercrime in the crypto space.

item.title: The latest Galaxy Z Flip 8 leak is a boring win for your pockets
item.description: Leaks about the Galaxy Z Flip 8 suggest it will have minimal changes compared to its predecessor, being slightly thinner. This may disappoint consumers expecting significant upgrades.

item.title: toksight added to PyPI
item.description: Toksight has been added to PyPI, offering a tokenizer analysis toolkit that allows users to compare various tokenization metrics across different models. This tool is valuable for developers working on NLP tasks.
